title: Build Systems That Test Space Hardware
description: Deepvac is looking for engineers who want to work across the full technical scope of thermal vacuum system development, from concept and subsystem design to integration and commissioning.

mission.title: Engineering Thermal Vacuum Systems for Demanding Applications
paragraphs: Deepvac develops advanced thermal vacuum systems for aerospace, research, and industrial test environments. Our work covers the full engineering scope of these systems, including mechanical design, thermal subsystems, vacuum architecture, control systems, instrumentation, and overall system integration.

We are building a team of engineers who are comfortable working across disciplines, who take ownership of technical decisions, and who care about the long-term performance and reliability of the systems they design.
values: Technical depth and sound engineering judgement
Ownership of technical decisions and outcomes
Willingness to work across system boundaries
Precision in design, documentation, and execution

items.title: System Concept Development
items.description: Translate customer requirements into technically sound system concepts for thermal vacuum and space simulation applications.

items.title: Subsystem Engineering
items.description: Design and specify vacuum, thermal, mechanical, control, and instrumentation subsystems, including interfaces and component selection.

items.title: Integration & Commissioning
items.description: Support build-up, acceptance testing, troubleshooting, and commissioning of complete systems in real project environments.

items.title: Documentation & Reviews
items.description: Create engineering documentation, calculations, specifications, and design review material with a focus on clarity and reliability.

items.title: Work on Real Hardware
items.description: Design, build, and commission physical test systems that support aerospace qualification, research missions, and demanding development programs.

items.title: Engineering Ownership
items.description: Take responsibility for technical decisions in a small team where engineering quality, speed, and accountability matter.

items.title: Multidisciplinary Scope
items.description: Work across mechanical, thermal, vacuum, electrical, and software-related interfaces within one integrated system.

items.title: Direct Customer Impact
items.description: Your work directly enables test campaigns for aerospace and research customers and has visible impact on project outcomes.

fit.title: Engineers Who Thrive at Deepvac
paragraphs: Deepvac is a strong fit for engineers who want to work on technically demanding systems with real-world constraints and real operational consequences. The work requires structured thinking, practical problem-solving, and the willingness to engage beyond a single discipline.

We are especially interested in people who value engineering substance over presentation, who can work independently when needed, and who are motivated by building systems that must perform reliably in demanding environments.
fit.fitTitle: You are likely a strong fit if
fitPoints: You enjoy solving practical engineering problems on real hardware.
You are comfortable working across disciplines and technical interfaces.
You can move between analysis, design, implementation, and troubleshooting.
You communicate clearly and make technically grounded decisions.
You care about documentation, reliability, and long-term system performance.

opportunities.title: Opportunities at Deepvac
opportunities.description: We are currently building our team and welcome applications from engineers with relevant technical backgrounds who want to contribute in Garbsen, Germany.

generalApplication.title: General Application
generalApplication.description: We are always interested in hearing from engineers who want to contribute to the design, development, integration, and commissioning of advanced thermal vacuum systems. If there is no specific role listed that matches your background, we still encourage you to get in touch.
generalApplication.relevantBackgrounds: Relevant backgrounds may include mechanical engineering, thermal engineering, vacuum systems, controls, instrumentation, system integration, test engineering, or adjacent technical fields.
sendItems: A short note on your technical background

process.title: How We Hire
process.description: We aim for a straightforward and transparent process with clear communication throughout.

steps.title: Application Review
steps.description: We review your background, experience, and technical fit for the work we do at Deepvac.

steps.title: Introductory Conversation
steps.description: A first conversation to get to know each other, discuss your interests, and answer initial questions.

steps.title: Technical Discussion
steps.description: A focused exchange about your engineering experience, problem-solving approach, and relevant project work.

steps.title: On-Site Meeting
steps.description: A conversation in Garbsen to discuss the role in more detail and give you a better sense of the team and work environment.

steps.title: Decision & Next Steps
steps.description: We follow up quickly and communicate clearly about the outcome and potential next steps.

culture.title: How We Work
paragraphs: Deepvac operates at the intersection of engineering precision and entrepreneurial execution. We work in a hands-on environment where technical depth, cross-disciplinary collaboration, and personal ownership matter.
Engineers at Deepvac are expected to think beyond their immediate discipline, communicate clearly, and contribute to systems that must perform reliably in demanding real-world applications.
environmentItems: Small team with direct technical responsibility
Hands-on work across concept, design, integration, and commissioning
Close interaction with real projects, customers, and system-level decisions
Engineering culture focused on substance, clarity, and reliability

cta.title: Questions About Working at Deepvac?
cta.description: Reach out to learn more about the team, the work, and current opportunities.
